bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility package. The opponent’s “market-standard” message is cheap talk, and their actual offer gives me low utility, so I will counter with a strong bid anchored on my highest-value options across all issues.

I targeted a very high-utility bid for me, making only a tiny concession from my prior maximum offer because it is still very early at t=0.02. The opponent’s offers remain low utility for me and even worsened slightly on issueD, so their “authorized/final” language is not supported by meaningful concession behavior.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.04), while making a small concession on low-weight issueD to avoid simply repeating my prior offer. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and their “mandate” claims are cheap talk; the only concrete movement is a small improvement on issueC, so I will hold firm on my most important issues for now.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.83 because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain clustered near valueE with only a small move on issueC, and their messages about authorization are cheap talk unsupported by meaningful concession. I am conceding first on low-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making modest concessions on lower-weight issues rather than moving on issueA/issueB/issueC. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and their messages about authorization are cheap talk; their actual bids show only small, inconsistent movement, mostly around issueA/issueC while holding many valueE terms.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility bid around 0.76 while making some concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and their “authorization” messages are cheap talk, but their repeated use of valueE/valueD suggests I can test movement there while holding firm on my high-importance issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility while making meaningful concessions only on low-weight issues, since time is still very early at t=0.12. The opponent’s actual offers show limited but real movement on issueC toward my best value, while they keep pushing valueE elsewhere despite repeated “principal” claims, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA/issueB and concede issueD/issueE to valueE.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only modest concessions on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low for my utility and their messages about authorization are cheap talk, though their repeated use of issueD/valueD suggests that may be a plausible small concession area. I avoid repeating my previous bid by adjusting issueD while holding firm on my highest-value issues.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.75, since it is early in the negotiation at t=0.16 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers and messages mostly hold firm on low-utility terms for me, with only small movement on issueC/issueA/issueE, so I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issues D and E while protecting my most important issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.18 we are still early, so I am targeting a strong utility while making concessions mainly on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers and messages show repeated pressure toward low values on issueD/issueE and some movement to issueC:valueB, so I will hold firm on high-importance issueA and issueB while matching their latest signals on the lower-weight terms.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.75 because we are only at t=0.20, so it is too early to concede on my high-weight issues A, B, and C. The opponent’s offers remain low-value to me and their repeated “final authorization” messages are cheap talk, though their bidding suggests they care about valueE/valueD on lower-weight issues, so I concede there first.

I am targeting roughly 0.70 weighted utility: a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids, but still above any low-value package given we are only at t=0.22. The opponent’s offers remain very unfavorable to me and their “hard boundary” messages are not credible given their changing bids, but they repeatedly favor valueE/valueD on lower-weight issues, so I concede there while holding firm on my most important issueA and preferred issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.686, keeping firm on my most important issueA and high-value issueC while conceding on lower-cost issueE and maintaining the prior issueB concession. At t=0.24 there is no need to collapse toward the opponent’s low-utility offers, though their repeated issueC=valueB signals that this may be acceptable; their “principal authorization” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by varied offers.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.67, preserving my high-weight priorities on issueA and issueC while offering concessions on lower-weight issueD and issueE plus a partial concession on issueB. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low-utility proposals; their offers show only limited movement and their repeated “authorization” messages are cheap talk rather than binding evidence.

I am targeting a strong but slightly more flexible bid with utility about 0.686, since we are still early-mid at t=0.28 and should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim authorization limits, but their actual offers show movement, especially the latest concession to issueA=valueA; I will hold issueA and issueC, while conceding on lower-weight issueD/issueE and modestly on issueB.

At t=0.30 I am targeting a still-strong but meaningfully conceded utility around 0.65, since there is time left but the opponent has shown some movement, especially occasionally on issueA and issueC. I am holding firm on my highest-value issues issueA and issueC, while conceding on issueB, issueD, and issueE where the opponent has repeatedly proposed lower values; I treat their “final authorization” messages as cheap talk because their actual offers keep changing.

At t=0.32 I am still targeting a solid mid-game utility while making a concrete tradeoff based on the opponent’s latest movement. Their messages repeatedly claim hard limits, but the offer history shows real concessions, especially on issueB and issueC; I will accept most of their latest structure while holding firm on issueA, my highest-weight issue.

I am targeting a still-strong mid-game utility around 0.60, conceding on lower-priority issueD/issueE and partially on issueA while holding issueC at my top value and issueB at a good compromise. The opponent’s offers have improved in utility to me despite repeated “no further movement” claims, so I trust the bid history over the messages and make a reciprocal concession without dropping too far at t=0.34.

I am targeting a moderate concession bid with about 0.58 weighted utility, preserving value on the important issues B and C while accepting the opponent’s recent movement to issueA=valueB. At t=0.36 there is still time, but the opponent’s actual offers are slowly improving despite repeated “final mandate” language, so I will concede mainly on lower-weight issues D/E and avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.63, preserving my highest-weight gains on issueA and issueC while asking only a limited improvement on issueB. At t=0.38 there is still time, but the opponent’s offers have clearly improved despite repeated “final authorization” messages, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and make a feasible counter close to their latest package.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.665, holding firm on the highest-value issues for me (issueA=valueA and issueC=valueB) while conceding on lower/mid-weight areas the opponent has repeatedly pushed, especially issueB and issueD. At t=0.40 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low-utility package; their repeated “final authorization” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by their actual movement. This bid also incorporates signals from their recent offers, including issueB=valueD, issueD=valueE, and issueE=valueC, without repeating my last bid exactly.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.71 by holding firm on my most important issues, especially issueA and issueC, while moving on lower/medium-impact issues that the opponent’s recent offers suggest may matter to them. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim hard limits, but their actual bids have varied and recently included concessions like issueB:valueC and issueD:valueC, so I am responding to the bidding pattern rather than the cheap-talk constraints.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility of about 0.60 while making meaningful concessions on issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE compared with my ideal package. The opponent has steadily improved from very low-value offers and most recently signaled willingness on issueA=valueB and issueB=valueB, while their repeated “final/authorized” messages are not reliable because their actual bids keep changing. I am holding issueC=valueB because it is important to me and the opponent has previously offered it several times.
